contentSequences.reason: Simple heading is best as default content - an author would type this as an H2 in a document.

contentSequences.description: 7 featured article cards. Each card has: optional thumbnail image, date (e.g. 'Mar 17, 2026'), author name/link, and linked article title. Cards appear in a mixed layout with larger featured cards and smaller grid cards.
contentSequences.reason: Repeated card items with image + text content (date, author, title) map perfectly to the Cards block pattern. Each article preview becomes one row in the cards table: image in col1, text content (date, author, linked title) in col2.

contentModel.col1: Image (thumbnail of article, optional - some cards have no image)
contentModel.col2: Date line (e.g. 'Mar 17, 2026 | Microsoft Corporate Blogs'), then linked title as heading

contentSequences.reason: Simple heading is best as default content.

contentSequences.description: 8 recent article cards with thumbnail, date, author, and title. Smaller uniform card layout compared to featured section.
contentSequences.reason: Same card pattern as featured section. Repeated article previews with image + text content map to Cards block. Visual differences (smaller size) will be handled by CSS variant styling.

contentModel.col2: Date line (e.g. 'Feb 20, 2026 | Microsoft Corporate Blogs'), then linked title as heading

contentSequences.description: 'View More' call-to-action button linking to page 2
contentSequences.reason: Single CTA link is best as default content - an author would write this as a linked paragraph with button styling.

contentSequences.description: 'Follow us:' text with RSS subscription link
contentSequences.reason: Simple text with a link. Default content is sufficient.

contentModel.col2: Text content: date | author line, then linked article title as heading

summary.notes: The Microsoft Blog homepage has two main content sections (Featured Posts and More News), both using the Cards block pattern for article listings. The Featured section has 7 cards and the More News section has 8 cards plus a View More CTA. A small social footer section has RSS link as default content. Non-content to remove: UHF header/footer (handled by EDS header/footer blocks), cookie banner, search form, All Microsoft mega menu.
